title: oryzopsis coerulescens var. straminea
intro: Oryzopsis coerulescens var. straminea, also known as a variety of the blue wildrye plant, is a type of flowering plant native to mountainous regions in the western United States. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and reducing pain. The health benefits of Oryzopsis coerulescens var. straminea include its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever, as well as its potential to alleviate respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and grinding the roots or leaves to create teas or tinctures for medicinal use.
